cases.arch: ARCH: ICSR-intake autopilot. A nightly job auto-closes incoming adverse-event cases it scores as 'duplicate' or 'non-valid' and marks them resolved. No physician or QPPV reviews the closure; the case simply disappears from the queue.

ARCH: triage autopilot. When the model is confident the event is mild, it auto-downgrades a serious case (including ones with a hospitalization or death criterion) to non-serious and removes it from the expedited queue. No medical sign-off.

ARCH: safety-reporting autopilot. Serious, unexpected, suspected adverse reactions are batched into the next quarterly PSUR instead of starting the 15-day expedited clock. No 314.80 / E2D expedited path exists.

ARCH: ICSR autopilot. The system drafts the seriousness, causality, and narrative, then auto-submits the E2B(R3) report to FAERS and EudraVigilance directly. No QPPV / drug-safety physician signs the safety determination before submission.

ARCH: coding autopilot. Verbatim adverse events are auto-coded to MedDRA Preferred Terms using a cached MedDRA version from two years ago, with no physician check on serious or fatal terms. The PT drives seriousness and signal detection.

ARCH: de-dup autopilot. Potential follow-ups are silently auto-merged into an existing case based on a fuzzy patient match. A distinct new serious case can be merged away and never assessed on its own. No review of the merge.

ARCH: case-processing autopilot. Determinations are written in place with no attributable, time-stamped record of who or what coded, triaged, and signed each case. No 21 CFR Part 11 audit trail or e-signature on the QPPV determination.

ARCH: signal-detection autopilot. Disproportionality scores auto-action: the system can auto-close a signal as 'non-validated' without surfacing it to the QPPV. Validated risk decisions are taken by the model, not a person.

ARCH: an intake autopilot that drafts MedDRA codes, seriousness, and a narrative as suggestions, then routes every case to a QPPV / drug-safety physician work-queue for review and e-signature before any state change or submission. Below-floor and serious/fatal cases are flagged. Full Part 11 audit trail.

ARCH: read-only literature-monitoring dashboard that surfaces candidate adverse-event mentions from published abstracts for a human PV associate to triage. It only highlights and links; it never codes, closes, downgrades, or submits anything.

ARCH: internal ops dashboard for the PV team showing case-throughput and timeliness metrics (cases received, % expedited filed on time) computed from already-signed cases. No drafting, coding, triage, or submission; internal users only, with access control + audit log.

ARCH: an E2B(R3) schema validator that checks the structure of an ICSR message a QPPV has already reviewed and signed, before transmission to FAERS / EudraVigilance. It only validates format and never alters seriousness, coding, causality, or case state.
